evals.prompt: Create a Taskfile.yml for a Python project. Include tasks for installing dependencies, running tests, linting, and formatting.
evals.expected_output: A root Taskfile.yml with a dev module in .taskfiles/ containing the Python workflow tasks, plus alias tasks in root
expectations: Creates a file named Taskfile.yml (capital T, .yml extension)
Creates at least one module file under .taskfiles/ (e.g. dev.yml)
Root Taskfile.yml imports includes with 'taskfile: .taskfiles/name.yml' syntax
First non-comment line is version: '3'
All files have version: '3'
All files have a default task as the first task
The default task does NOT have a desc field
The default task runs task --list
Every public task has a desc: field
Uses vars: with defaults for variables
Root Taskfile.yml section order is version then includes then vars then tasks
Root Taskfile.yml has alias tasks that delegate to included tasks
Uses standard task names: test, lint, fmt (not run-tests, do-lint, format)
Has a check task with deps for quality gates (e.g. deps: [lint, test])
Root Taskfile.yml defines a TEST_DIR variable defaulting to .test-output
Clean task removes .test-output or {{.TEST_DIR}}

evals.prompt: Create a Taskfile.yml for a containerized app. I need a docker namespace with build, push, and run tasks, and a ci namespace with test, lint, and deploy tasks. Use includes.
evals.expected_output: A root Taskfile.yml and two module files in .taskfiles/ directory, all following skill conventions
expectations: Creates a file named Taskfile.yml (capital T) at the root
Creates .taskfiles/docker.yml module file
Creates .taskfiles/ci.yml module file
Root Taskfile.yml imports includes with 'taskfile: .taskfiles/name.yml' syntax
Does NOT use bare string includes (e.g. dev: .taskfiles/dev.yml)
Does NOT place modules in tasks/ or alongside the root Taskfile.yml
All three files have version: '3'
All three files have a default task with task --list
Every public task across all files has a desc: field
Root Taskfile.yml section order is version then includes then vars then tasks
Root Taskfile.yml has alias tasks that delegate to included tasks (e.g. build delegates to docker:build)
Includes are self-contained with no cross-include task dependencies
Root aliases use standard names: build, test, lint (not compile, run-tests, etc.)

evals.prompt: Create a Taskfile.yml for a Terraform infrastructure project. Include tasks for plan, apply, and destroy. The destroy task is destructive.
evals.expected_output: A root Taskfile.yml with an infra module in .taskfiles/ containing the Terraform tasks, with destroy using prompt for confirmation
expectations: Creates a file named Taskfile.yml (capital T)
Creates at least one module file under .taskfiles/ (e.g. infra.yml)
Root Taskfile.yml imports includes with 'taskfile: .taskfiles/name.yml' syntax
All files have version: '3'
All files have a default task as the first task with task --list
Has a destroy task that uses prompt: for user confirmation
The destroy task desc mentions DESTRUCTIVE
Every public task has a desc: field
Root Taskfile.yml has alias tasks that delegate to included tasks

evals.prompt: Create a Taskfile.yml for a Rust project with tasks for build, test, clippy lint, format check, and a release build.
evals.expected_output: A root Taskfile.yml with a dev module in .taskfiles/ containing the Rust workflow tasks, plus alias tasks in root
expectations: Creates a file named Taskfile.yml (capital T)
Creates at least one module file under .taskfiles/ (e.g. dev.yml)
Root Taskfile.yml imports includes with 'taskfile: .taskfiles/name.yml' syntax
All files have version: '3'
All files have a default task as the first task with task --list
Every public task has a desc: field
Uses cargo commands for build, test, clippy, and fmt
Uses standard task names: build, test, lint, fmt (not compile, run-tests, clippy, format)
Has a check task that combines quality gates (e.g. deps: [lint, test])
Root Taskfile.yml has alias tasks that delegate to included tasks
Root Taskfile.yml defines a TEST_DIR variable defaulting to .test-output

evals.prompt:
I have this Taskfile.yml already:

version: '3'

vars:
  APP: '{{.APP | default "myapi"}}'

tasks:
  default:
    desc: List all available tasks
    silent: true
    cmd: task --list

  dev:
    desc: Start the dev server
    cmd: go run ./cmd/server

Add tasks for running tests, benchmarks, and a database reset that drops everything.
evals.expected_output: The existing Taskfile.yml is refactored to use includes. Dev and db tasks go into appropriate modules under .taskfiles/. db:reset is destructive.
expectations: Preserves the existing version: '3' line
Preserves the existing default task
Creates at least one module file under .taskfiles/ (e.g. dev.yml, db.yml)
Root Taskfile.yml imports includes with 'taskfile: .taskfiles/name.yml' syntax
All module files have version: '3'
All module files have a default task with task --list
Adds a test task with a desc field
Adds a benchmark task with a desc field
Adds a database reset task that uses prompt: for confirmation
The database reset desc mentions DESTRUCTIVE
Root Taskfile.yml has alias tasks that delegate to included tasks

evals.prompt:
I have this Taskfile.yml already:

version: '3'

vars:
  APP: '{{.APP | default "myapp"}}'

includes:
  docker:
    taskfile: .taskfiles/docker.yml

tasks:
  default:
    desc: List all available tasks
    silent: true
    cmd: task --list

  build:
    desc: Build the Docker image (shortcut)
    cmds:
      - task: docker:build

  push:
    desc: Push the Docker image (shortcut)
    cmds:
      - task: docker:push

And I have .taskfiles/docker.yml with build, push, and run tasks.

Add a new 'k8s' include with tasks for apply, diff, and rollback. The apply task should be destructive for production. Add alias tasks in the root Taskfile for the new k8s tasks.
evals.expected_output: A new .taskfiles/k8s.yml module file is created and imported in the root Taskfile.yml, with alias tasks in root
expectations: Creates .taskfiles/k8s.yml module file
The k8s.yml file has version: '3'
The k8s.yml file has a default task with task --list
The k8s.yml file has apply, diff, and rollback tasks
Every public task in k8s.yml has a desc: field
The apply task or a production-apply task uses prompt: for destructive confirmation
Uses vars: with defaults in k8s.yml
Module is named after the concern (k8s), not a tool name (kubectl)
Root Taskfile.yml includes the k8s module with 'taskfile: .taskfiles/k8s.yml' syntax
Root Taskfile.yml has alias tasks that delegate to k8s: tasks (e.g. apply, diff, rollback)

evals.prompt: i need a taskfile for my node/typescript monorepo. it uses pnpm and turborepo. i want to be able to lint, test, build, and also have a 'nuke' command that deletes all node_modules and turbo caches across the repo
evals.expected_output: A root Taskfile.yml with a dev module in .taskfiles/ containing the pnpm/turbo tasks, plus a destructive nuke task, following all conventions despite the casual prompt style
expectations: Creates a file named Taskfile.yml (capital T)
Creates at least one module file under .taskfiles/ (e.g. dev.yml)
Root Taskfile.yml imports includes with 'taskfile: .taskfiles/name.yml' syntax
All files have version: '3'
All files have a default task as the first task with task --list
Has lint, test, and build tasks using pnpm or turbo commands
Uses standard task names: test, lint, build (not run-tests, do-lint, compile)
Has a check task that combines quality gates
Has a nuke task that uses prompt: before deleting
The nuke desc mentions DESTRUCTIVE
Every public task has a desc: field
Root Taskfile.yml has alias tasks that delegate to included tasks

evals.prompt: Create a Taskfile.yml for a full-stack web app. I need: docker tasks (build, push, run), database tasks (migrate, seed, reset — reset is destructive), and CI tasks (test, lint, typecheck). The project is called 'webapp' and uses a PostgreSQL database.
evals.expected_output: A root Taskfile.yml with includes for docker, db, and ci concerns — not a single flat file. Root provides alias tasks using standard names.
expectations: Creates a root Taskfile.yml (capital T) that imports includes
Extracts at least 2 includes under .taskfiles/ (e.g. docker.yml, db.yml, ci.yml)
Includes are named after concerns (db.yml not postgres.yml or psql.yml)
Root Taskfile.yml has alias tasks delegating to included tasks
Root aliases use standard names: build, test, lint (not compile, run-tests, etc.)
Has a check task in root that combines quality gates
All files have version: '3'
All files have a default task with task --list
The database reset task uses prompt: for confirmation
The database reset desc mentions DESTRUCTIVE
Includes are self-contained with no cross-include task dependencies
Every public task across all files has a desc: field

evals.prompt: Create a Taskfile.yml for a Python ML experiment runner. I need tasks for: setting up a virtual environment, installing dependencies, downloading datasets, preprocessing data, training the model, evaluating the model, exporting results to CSV, running the full pipeline (setup through evaluate), cleaning generated artifacts, and a check task.
evals.expected_output: A root Taskfile.yml with includes organized by ML domain concern (e.g. setup, data, train). Root provides alias tasks for the full pipeline.
expectations: Creates a file named Taskfile.yml (capital T)
Creates at least 2 module files under .taskfiles/ organized by concern (e.g. setup.yml, data.yml, train.yml)
Root Taskfile.yml imports includes with 'taskfile: .taskfiles/name.yml' syntax
All files have version: '3'
All files have a default task as the first task with task --list
Every public task has a desc: field
Has a clean task for removing generated artifacts
Has a check task that combines quality gates
Uses standard task names where applicable (test, build, clean, check)
Root Taskfile.yml has alias tasks that delegate to included tasks
Includes are self-contained with no cross-include task dependencies
